quote: If other judges rule the same way, and hold companies responsible for the nonsense their chatbots say, one of the biggest alleged use cases for large language models could dry up, fast.
claim: If courts hold companies liable for chatbot confabulations, the customer service use case for LLMs could collapse

quote: an Air Canada chatbot confabulated ("hallucinated") an answer – telling a customer that bereavement trips would be refunded
claim: LLM-powered chatbots confabulate answers in customer service contexts, making up policies that do not exist

quote: We are about to have a whole generation of children educated by fake videos that are completely plausible to naive audiences yet biologically (or physically or culturally, etc) incorrect.
claim: AI-generated video will produce a generation of children exposed to plausible but biologically, physically, or culturally incorrect educational content

quote: In short order, with Sora or some opensource counterparts that will emerge, scammers will start to make photorealistic fake videos simply to sell ads.
claim: Scammers will soon use Sora-like tools to create photorealistic fake videos for ad revenue

quote: A major educational resource will likely be spoiled practically overnight.
claim: YouTube as an educational resource will be degraded by AI-generated fake videos

quote: Probably the biggest single problem with large language models is that they make things up
claim: The biggest single problem with LLMs is confabulation

quote: Turns out Sora makes stuff up, too
claim: Sora exhibits the same confabulation problem as LLMs, but in the visual domain

quote: The error is not an error of insufficient computational power
claim: Sora's errors in rendering chess boards and similar artifacts are not due to insufficient compute

quote: Nowadays, we have "multimodal" systems, trained on both text and images, and the aforementioned "hallucinations" … and other errors persist and even crop up in new ways.
claim: Adding multimodal grounding (images + text) has not solved LLM confabulation problems

quote: Sora is failing to apprehend a cultural regularity of the world, despite ample evidence
claim: Sora fails to learn cultural regularities like the rules of chess despite ample training data

quote: The system is trying to approximate the world, but it just isn't very good at that job. It uses arrangements of pixels to predict other arrangements of pixels, but it does not try to build an internal model of physics, and it does not try to build an internal model of cultural artifacts, either.
claim: Sora approximates pixel arrangements rather than building internal models of physics or cultural artifacts

quote: In most or all of the errors we have seen thus far, virtually every bit of the images are locally correct; if you just looked at a tiny patch of a given flawed video in isolation, for a single moment, you wouldn't notice anything wrong. What's missing is any global comprehension by the system.
claim: Sora's outputs are locally correct at the pixel level but lack global comprehension of scenes

quote: From AGI perspective, however, this is an immense warning sign. The factuality problems that have haunted LLMs are present even in a new architecture trained on ungodly amounts of data.
claim: Sora's factuality problems despite massive data are a warning sign that new architectures and more data do not solve confabulation for AGI

quote: In my view, these claims — about AGI and world models — are hyperbolic, and unlikely to be true.
claim: Claims that Sora represents progress toward AGI or true world models are hyperbolic and unlikely to be true

quote: OpenAI wants us to believe that this is a "path towards building general purpose simulations of the physical world". As it turns out, that's either hype or confused
claim: OpenAI's claim that Sora is a path to general-purpose physical world simulation is hype or confused

quote: children may be born with object permanence, and certainly have some control it by the age of 4 or 5 months; Sora is never really getting it, even with mountains and mountains of data.
claim: Sora fails at basic object permanence despite massive training data, something human infants achieve by 4-5 months

quote: I predict that many will be hard to remedy. Why? Because the glitches don't stem from the data, they stem from a flaw in how the system reconstructs reality.
claim: Sora's physics glitches will be hard to fix because they stem from fundamental flaws in how the system reconstructs reality, not from data insufficiency

quote: these glitches are in some ways akin to LLM "hallucinations", artifacts from (roughly speaking) decompression from lossy compression. They don't derive from the world.
claim: Sora's physics glitches are analogous to LLM hallucinations -- artifacts of lossy compression rather than reflections of training data

quote: More data won't solve that problem. And like other generative AI systems, there is no way to encode (and guarantee) constraints like "be truthful" or "obey the laws of physics" or "don't just invent (or eliminate) objects".
claim: More data will not solve Sora's physics problems, and there is no way to guarantee constraints like truthfulness or physics compliance in generative AI

quote: Indeed the real lesson here is that Generative AI remains a recalcitrant beast, no matter how much data you throw at it.
claim: Generative AI remains fundamentally unreliable regardless of data scale

quote: Sora is not a solution to AI's longstanding woes with space, time, and causality.
claim: Sora does not solve AI's longstanding problems with space, time, and causality

quote: If a system can't at all handle with the permanence of objects, I am not sure we should even call it a world model at all.
claim: A system that cannot handle object permanence should not be called a world model

quote: Sora is fantastic, but it is akin to morphing and splicing, rather than a path to the physical reasoning we would need for AGI. It is a model of how images change over time, not a model of what entities do in the world.
claim: Sora models how images change over time, not how entities behave in the world, making it irrelevant to AGI

quote: As a solution to artificial general intelligence, though, I see it as a distraction.
claim: Sora is a distraction from, not a path toward, AGI

quote: The need for altogether different technologies that are less opaque, more interpretable, more maintanable, and more debuggable — and hence more tractable—remains paramount.
claim: AI needs fundamentally different technologies that are less opaque, more interpretable, more maintainable, and more debuggable than current LLMs

quote: Generative AI is a kind of alchemy
claim: Generative AI development is a kind of alchemy -- tinkering without deep understanding

quote: these systems have never been been stable. Nobody has ever been able to engineer safety guarantees around then.
claim: Generative AI systems have never been stable and nobody has been able to engineer safety guarantees around them

quote: The AI we have got now isn't really up to the job. Getting the delicate balance here right is way outside current AI's wheelhouse.
claim: Current AI is not capable of balancing diversity representation with historical accuracy in image generation

quote: What one wants is a system that can distinguish past from future, acknowledging history while also playing a role in moving towards a more positive future. And one wants the system to have a nuanced understanding of what is even been asked. (That's the clearly lacking now).
claim: Current AI systems lack the nuanced understanding needed to distinguish historical from contemporary contexts in queries

quote: Some things have scaled exponentially, and some haven't. The capacity to endow machines with the commonsense understanding of history, culture, and human values that would be required to make sensible guardrails has not.
claim: The ability to give AI commonsense understanding of history, culture, and human values has not scaled with compute and data

quote: Maybe, and I am just spitballing, LLMs aren't quite the magic panacea that the industry lead us all to believe?
claim: LLMs are not the transformative panacea the industry has claimed

quote: Sora's can't reliably handle basic physics, ChatGPT had an unexplained meltdown, Gemini can't even remember who was on Apollo 11, there are no formal guarantees that any of this will ever work
claim: There are no formal guarantees that generative AI systems will ever work reliably

quote: The road from research idea to reliable production remains steep. RAG may reduce hallucinations somewhat, but it certainly won't come close to eliminating them.
claim: RAG may reduce LLM hallucinations somewhat but will not come close to eliminating them

quote: To make RAG work reliably would be to solve AGI.
claim: Making RAG work reliably for all queries is as hard as solving AGI

quote: To reliably address all queries, and not just some obvious ones like what was the recent Super Bowl score, would require solutions all the things the field has been struggling to do for 75 years without success.
claim: Reliably answering all queries via RAG would require solving problems AI has failed to solve for 75 years

quote: RAG would need to take natural language and visual input and deconstruct it a logical, computer interpretable form that you can check against databases, and reason over the results. That's really hard.
claim: For RAG to work reliably it would need to decompose natural language into logical forms and reason over them, which is extremely difficult

quote: Imagining perfectly reliable, hallucination-free chatbots by this time next year is bonkers.
claim: Perfectly reliable, hallucination-free chatbots will not exist by early 2025

quote: I do think AI will eventually work reliably. Some great-grandchild of RAG, perhaps extended, revised, and elaborated, might even help — if people seriously embrace neurosymbolic AI in coming years.
claim: AI will eventually work reliably, likely requiring neurosymbolic approaches rather than current LLM/RAG methods

quote: Making it work is very likely going to require a whole new technology, one that is unlikely to arrive until the tech industry gets over its infatuation with LLMs and quick fixes.
claim: Reliable AI will require a fundamentally new technology that will not arrive until the industry moves past its LLM fixation

quote: Waiting on RAG to fix what ails LLM is just waiting on another miracle.
claim: RAG is not going to fix the fundamental problems with LLMs

quote: sound advice for the particular circumstance (sternotomy) has been combined with generic advice (about ergonomics) that would sensible in other circumstances but deeply problematic here
claim: GenAI search engines produce dangerous medical errors by combining generically correct advice in contextually inappropriate ways (generative pastiche)

quote: New results from Subbarao Kambhapati cast doubts on the robustness of chain of thought prompting
claim: Chain-of-thought prompting is not robust, based on Kambhapati's research

quote: OpenAI as a whole no longer works according to the mission that was originally set out.
claim: OpenAI no longer operates according to its original nonprofit mission

quote: Sora has not learned physics, and is not a reliable guide to how the world works. It is at best a semi-reliable guide to how the world looks.
claim: Sora has not learned physics; it is at best a semi-reliable guide to how the world looks, not how it works

quote: These two—how the world works, and how the world looks—are fundamentally different.
claim: Modeling how the world looks (pixel prediction) is fundamentally different from modeling how the world works (physics)

quote: Sora absolutely fails at object permanence 101. People, animals, limbs, etc routinely wink in and out of existence willy-nilly, as a function of predictions about pixels, rather than as the computations of the states of physical entities.
claim: Sora fails at basic object permanence because it predicts pixels rather than computing states of physical entities

quote: It is precisely because Sora is not a physics engine that a large fraction of Sora's videos are inconsistent with physics or biology, in very basic ways.
claim: A large fraction of Sora's videos are inconsistent with basic physics or biology because Sora is not a physics engine

quote: Whenever there is a conflict between physics and image sequence prediction, image sequence prediction wins. The laws of physics are about objects, not pixels; Sora knows only about pixels and image spaces, not objects.
claim: When physics conflicts with image prediction in Sora, image prediction always wins because Sora operates on pixels, not objects

quote: humanity has now taken a back seat to money; their choices now seem very directly anchored in a need to generate a massive return, in direct contradiction to their promises to California, to Musk, and to the world.
claim: OpenAI now prioritizes profit over its original humanitarian mission, contradicting its promises

trigger: Hofmann et al. covert racism paper
quote: LLMs are, as I have been trying to tell you, too stupid to understand concepts like people and race; their fealty to superficial statistics drives this horrific stereotyping
claim: LLMs lack genuine understanding of concepts like people and race; their reliance on superficial statistics drives covert racist stereotyping

trigger: Hofmann et al. covert racism paper
quote: scale actually makes the covert racism worse
claim: Scaling up LLMs makes covert racism worse, not better

trigger: Hofmann et al. covert racism paper
quote: I honestly don't see an easy fix — simply adding human feedback made things worse, as the authors showed. And Google's debacle shows that guardrails are never easy.
claim: There is no easy fix for covert racism in LLMs; RLHF makes it worse and guardrails are unreliable

trigger: Hofmann et al. covert racism paper
quote: the LLM companies should recall their systems until they can find an adequate solution
claim: LLM companies should recall their systems until covert racism is adequately addressed

trigger: Hofmann et al. covert racism paper
quote: I would encourage Congress, the EEOC, HUD, the FTC, and others to investigate, and demand user logs and interaction data from the major LLM manufacturers.
claim: US government agencies should investigate LLM covert racism and demand user interaction data from LLM manufacturers

quote: it shows decisively that OpenAI knew for years that they had no intention of being as open as their name or public statements suggested.
claim: OpenAI knew for years it had no intention of being as open as its name and public statements suggested

quote: the apparent copyright infringements that Reid Southen and I discussed in early January continue apace two months later
claim: OpenAI's apparent copyright infringement in image generation continues unaddressed

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: Scaling has undeniably helped, but not solved any of the problems I pointed to above.
claim: Scaling has helped AI performance but has not solved problems of comprehension, factuality, stability, or reasoning

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: We are still a long way from machines that can genuinely understand human language
claim: Current AI systems are still far from genuinely understanding human language

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: we nowhere near the ordinary day-to-day intelligence of Rosey the Robot, a science-fiction housekeeper that could not only interpret a wide variety of human requests but safely act on them in real time.
claim: Current AI is nowhere near the general-purpose domestic robot intelligence depicted in science fiction

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: In time we will see that deep learning was only a tiny part of what we need to build if we're ever going to get trustworthy AI
claim: Deep learning will turn out to be only a small part of what is needed for trustworthy AI

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: Deep learning, which is fundamentally a technique for recognizing patterns, is at its best when all we need are rough-ready results, where stakes are low and perfect results optional
claim: Deep learning is best suited for low-stakes tasks where approximate results are acceptable

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: Current deep-learning systems frequently succumb to stupid errors.
claim: Current deep learning systems frequently make basic errors

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: scaling laws aren't universal laws like gravity but rather mere observations that might not hold forever, much like Moore's law
claim: AI scaling laws are empirical observations that may not hold indefinitely, analogous to Moore's law

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: I still think we need a paradigm shift. And it is increasingly looking like LLMs on their own are not the answer to AGI
claim: LLMs alone are not the answer to AGI; a paradigm shift is needed

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: the measures that have scaled have not captured what we desperately need to improve: genuine comprehension
claim: The metrics that have improved with scaling do not capture genuine comprehension

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: Toxic language can easily be elicited by jailbreaks, but less so in ordinary use; misinformation is still common, even without jailbreaks
claim: LLM toxicity has improved in normal use but remains elicitable via jailbreaks; misinformation remains common even without jailbreaks

trigger: 2-year anniversary of Marcus's 'Deep Learning Is Hitting a Wall' paper
quote: people like Bill Gates, Demis Hassabis, Yann LeCun, and Sam Altman have all recognized that a plateau might be coming
claim: Even prominent AI leaders (Gates, Hassabis, LeCun, Altman) have recognized that AI scaling may be hitting a plateau

quote: why $7T, if the ROI isn't yet clear?
claim: The ROI on generative AI investment is not yet clear despite massive spending

trigger: Retracted rat paper with AI-generated images
quote: GenAI is messing with science. And it may get a lot worse.
claim: Generative AI is degrading scientific integrity and the problem may worsen significantly

trigger: Retracted rat paper with AI-generated images
quote: the long term consequences on science may be severe
claim: The long-term consequences of LLM use in scientific papers may be severe

trigger: Retracted rat paper with AI-generated images
quote: GenAI-created garbage could quickly overwhelm their reviewing processes, leading to a precipitous drop in reputation. Everyone in science loses.
claim: GenAI-generated garbage submissions could overwhelm journal review processes, damaging scientific publishing reputation

trigger: Mira Murati WSJ interview on Sora training data
quote: Aside from all that, Murati claimed she had no idea what specific data Sora was trained on; given her role as CTO that seems all but inconceivable.
claim: OpenAI CTO Mira Murati's claim of not knowing what data Sora was trained on is not credible given her role

trigger: Mira Murati WSJ interview on Sora training data
quote: Half truths about openness, about what data they are training on, about finances, about their views on regulation, about when if ever they would pause, and more, essentially every topic that matters, from the day they were launched all the way to the present. A lack of full candor seems to run straight through the corporate DNA.
claim: A pattern of half-truths and lack of candor on openness, data, finances, and regulation runs through OpenAI's corporate DNA

trigger: Discovery of ChatGPT remnant phrases in scientific papers
quote: every article with ChatGPT remnants should be considered suspect and perhaps retracted, because hallucinations may have filtered in, and both authors and reviewers) were asleep at the switch.
claim: Every scientific article containing ChatGPT remnant phrases should be considered suspect and possibly retracted because hallucinations may have infiltrated the content

trigger: Discovery of ChatGPT remnant phrases in scientific papers
quote: Reviewers are typically unpaid academics who are already stretched to their limits; tripling their workload would not be feasible. And GenAI might do a lot worse than merely tripling workloads; the total number of articles may radically spike, many of them dubious and a waste of reviewers' time.
claim: GenAI will cause a radical spike in dubious scientific paper submissions that overwhelms the already-strained peer review system

trigger: Discovery of ChatGPT remnant phrases in scientific papers
quote: If science journals and science itself are overrun by LLM-generated garbage, as now seems imminent, my agnostic "I can't quite tell yet if LLMs will ultimately be of net benefit to society" is going to switch to "shut it down if they can't fix this problem."
claim: Science journals being overrun by LLM-generated garbage is imminent and may tip the cost-benefit analysis of LLMs to net negative for society

trigger: Discovery of ChatGPT remnant phrases in scientific papers
quote: Literally the only other solution I can envision is to force LLM manufacturers to pay a significant tax to subsidize the journal review system that they are about to destroy.
claim: LLM manufacturers should be taxed to subsidize the journal review system that their products are degrading
